title: Strobilanthes extensa
taxonomy: Strobilanthes extensa belongs to the family Acanthaceae. This classification places it within the order Lamiales and the clade Asterids. The genus Strobilanthes is a group of flowering plants characterized by their unique inflorescences. Within the family Acanthaceae, Strobilanthes extensa is part of the tribe Ruvoideae. The species is native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia.

intro: Strobilanthes extensa, also known as Blue Pilea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and inflammation, due to its purported health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Strobilanthes extensa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, Strobilanthes extensa is commonly prepared as a decoction or a powder, and is also used in the formulation of herbal teas and capsules.
